papers.claim: LLMs are more defensible as interaction and explainability layers around optimization workflows than as core optimization-method evidence.
papers.evidence: Deferred as future-facing context; reading-card basis reports moderate certainty (sufficiency 0.62) and non-core fit for MILP formulation/solution quality.

papers.claim: A discussion-ready pattern is to keep integrated MILP as the decision backbone and use RL/heuristics to improve tractability.
papers.evidence: Kept due to end-to-end integrated MILP scope (production, transport, storage, shipping) plus PPO and relax-and-fix extensions; sufficiency 0.78.

papers.claim: Constraint learning can extend MILP/MIO under partial observability while preserving optimization structure for coordinated decisions.
papers.evidence: Kept with strong relevance; rationale cites learned constraints/objectives embedded in optimization and trust-region/ensemble mechanisms; sufficiency 0.87.

papers.claim: Production-inventory MILP with explicit capacity, shortage, and line-switch constraints provides a practical baseline for discussing integrated planning trade-offs.
papers.evidence: Kept based on case-validated CPLEX implementation and reusable coordinated planning structure, with explicit note that full distribution integration is limited.

papers.claim: Learning-assisted MILP can deliver large runtime gains with explicit guarantee-vs-speed trade-offs, which is central to discussion of advanced extensions.
papers.evidence: Kept with reported 4.3x speedup under guarantees and 10.2x without guarantees, while retaining exact optimization core; sufficiency 0.88.

papers.claim: Logistics reliability and delay KPIs should be used in discussion to justify realistic assumptions, not as optimization-method evidence.
papers.evidence: Deferred because it provides contextual grounding but no explicit MILP/stochastic/RL prescriptive formulation.

papers.claim: A MILP-first thesis discussion can be strengthened by a formal screening lens for ML-assisted combinatorial optimization methods.
papers.evidence: Kept for methodological framing of prediction-and-optimization, policy learning, and ML-guided heuristics, with caution on limited domain specificity; sufficiency 0.78.

papers.claim: Coupling demand forecasting with dynamic inventory control supports prediction-and-optimization arguments in discussion, but as an extension rather than a core integrated model.
papers.evidence: Kept as advanced-method reference showing LR/RF/SVR with dynamic (t,R,S) control and gains versus EOQ; scope limitation acknowledged; sufficiency 0.82.

papers.claim: ML augmentation of branch-and-bound components is a practical post-formulation path to accelerate MILP workflows.
papers.evidence: Kept as methodological evidence on branching/cuts/node selection/heuristics/configuration acceleration, with non-domain-specific positioning; sufficiency 0.66.

papers.claim: Iterative prediction-and-optimization can preserve MILP rigor while handling endogenous demand-network feedback at larger scales.
papers.evidence: Kept with ANN+MILP iterative framework and two-stage heuristic scaling reported as strongly relevant to baseline-plus-extension thesis structure; sufficiency 0.86.

papers.claim: Strategic network design optimization with cooperation dynamics broadens discussion beyond operational planning while remaining compatible with MILP-style reasoning.
papers.evidence: Kept based on formal objective/constraint model solved by branch-and-bound and case-reported cost reduction; strategic scope noted; sufficiency 0.78.

papers.claim: Prescriptive ML is best positioned in discussion as a repeated-solve enhancement that complements MILP/MICO baselines via improved feasibility behavior.
papers.evidence: Kept with high relevance to advanced-method track and adequate evidence quality (sufficiency 0.84, moderate uncertainty).

papers.claim: Integrated location-production-inventory-distribution optimization is a core anchor for arguing the value of coordinated versus sequential planning.
papers.evidence: Kept as direct thesis-core match with exact integrated modeling, integrated-vs-sequential benchmarking, and practical extensions; anchors_count 6 and sufficiency 0.86.
